title: Primitive Types
subtitle: The basic building blocks
goal: Learn the basic data types in JavaScript.
explanation: Every piece of information in JavaScript has a type.
Numbers are used for mathematical calculations.
Boolean has only two values: true or false.
null means an intentionally empty value.
undefined means the value has not been set yet.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Data types are like different kinds of LEGO bricks. They look different, but your whole program is built from them.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often confuse null and undefined. They are different values with different purposes.

quiz.question: Which type is used for text?
